events.title: Toronto shooting: two dead and four injured at Salsa on St Clair street festival
events.description: Police say two people exchanged gunfire in shooting that mayor called an ‘irresponsible act of violence’ in festival attended by families A shooting near a Toronto street festival killed two men and wounded four other people on Saturday evening, police said, adding that what initially prompted an active-shooter warning was an exchange of gunfire between two people targeting each other. Toronto police deputy chief Frank Barredo said investigators recovered two firearms after the shooting, which was reported at 8.12pm near St. Clair Avenue West and Arlington Avenue, where the Salsa on St Clair festival was underway. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Toronto shooting: two dead and four injured at Salsa on St Clair street festival

events.title: Venezuela quake death toll passes 4,300 as scale of recovery effort looms large
events.description: Nearly 17,000 injured and thousands more listed as missing amid calls by president Delcy Rodríguez and UN for financial help The death toll in Venezuela’s devastating twin earthquakes has passed 4,300, the government said on Saturday. At least 4,333 people were killed and 16,740 injured in the back-to-back quakes on 24 June that flattened entire districts in the coastal state of La Guaira, the Venezuelan parliament chief, Jorge Rodríguez, wrote on Telegram. Thousands more people are listed as missing. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venezuela quake death toll passes 4,300 as scale of recovery effort looms large

events.title: World News in Brief: UN spotlights education aid solution, Sri Lanka prison violence, humanitarian aid to Venezuela
events.description: The UN Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) urged international lenders to expand debt-for-education swaps, warning in a Friday report that many developing countries are spending more on debt servicing than on schooling their children.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: UN spotlights education aid solution, Sri Lanka prison violence, humanitarian aid to Venezuela

events.title: Weather tracker: Typhoon leaves people stranded on rooftops in China
events.description: Eleven reported dead as flooding also brings danger of snakes, while buildings collapse in Mumbai amid heavy rain As the first typhoon to make landfall in China for the 2026 season, Maysak has caused devastating damage in southern and central regions. The Guangxi region received intense downpours of up to 280mm in 12 hours, causing rivers to swell and dam walls to break. By Monday morning, flooding across the city of Nanning and surrounding areas had resulted in many people being stranded on rooftops. Flood waters pose additional threats in China because of the presence of wild and farmed snakes. On Thursday local media reported that hundreds of snakes , including cobras, had escaped from flooded breeding farms. Typhoon Maysak also aided the development of two destructive tornados that swept across central China later on Monday evening. This occurred when warm air from the south, brought up by Typhoon Maysak, collided with cold air in the north. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: Typhoon leaves people stranded on rooftops in China

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Open debate on sexual violence in conflict
events.description: The UN Security Council is holding an open debate Tuesday honouring the promise of international law to survivors of sexual violence in conflict as more reports emerge about warring parties using rape as a weapon, actions that constitute war crimes. Stay tuned for live updates from our meetings coverage team.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Open debate on sexual violence in conflict

events.title: Venomous snakes escape breeding farms in southern China during flooding
events.description: Local media in Hengzhou report king ratsnakes and cobras among hundreds in flood waters caused by typhoon Maysak Hundreds of snakes, including cobras, have escaped from flooded breeding farms in southern China as severe storms continue to batter parts of the country. State media reported that a snake farm in Hengzhou, in Guangxi Zhuang autonomous region, was hit by flood waters after days of heavy rainfall caused by typhoon Maysak , prompting warnings for nearby residents. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venomous snakes escape breeding farms in southern China during flooding

events.title: Nigeria says army has killed 300 bandits in north-western state of Zamfara
events.description: Vigilantes also took part in the fight that raged all night and the following morning, residents say Nigerian soldiers killed more than 300 members of kidnapping and cattle bandit gangs in the north-western state of Zamfara this week, according to a government official. Government troops targeted the gangs in Gummi district in a two-day operation that “led to the elimination of more than 300 terrorists”, Zamfara’s information commissioner, Mahmud Muhammad Dantawasa, said in a statement. events.title: First patients enrolled in record-breaking Ebola treatment trial in DRC
events.description: Two drugs are being trialled in the Ituri region in a programme set up just six weeks after the outbreak was declared, with hopes it will reduce mortality rates There is no approved drug to help the medical teams scrabbling to save lives in the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo – but there are hopes that could change within months as the first patients are enrolled in a treatment trial. It is a record pace to set up and start this kind of research, scientists said, with patients enrolled just six weeks after the outbreak being declared a public health emergency of international concern by the World Health Organization (WHO) on 17 May. Continue reading...

events.title: Global population at 8.3 billion: What does that mean for anyone under 30?
events.description: With the world population surpassing 8.3 billion today, can we truly realize the hopes and dreams of the largest generation of young people ever? On World Population Day 2026, the spotlight is on empowering youth to shape their futures through education, health, and opportunity. From family planning to sustainable development, this global observance asks a vital question: are we investing enough in tomorrow's leaders today? Jean-Emile Jammine speaks with Diene Keita, Executive Director of the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA).

events.title: Furore in Nigeria over fake federal agency set up in government HQ
events.description: President orders investigation after fictitious body given funding, triggering renewed scrutiny of alleged corruption A fictitious federal entity that was allocated 1.3bn naira (£700,000) in Nigeria’s 2026 budget has precipitated a political storm in Africa’s largest democracy in the run-up to a general election set for January. The fake agency came to light last October when Femi Gbajabiamila, the president’s chief of staff, wrote to the police alleging that his signature, along with official seals and reference numbers, had been forged by Adeniyi Adeyemi Matthew, who was claiming to have been appointed by the presidency to head the presidential foreign intervention promotion council (PFIPC). Continue reading...

events.title: Mexico investigates whether US lied about role in capture of drug lord
events.description: Reporting suggests FBI involved in seizure of Ismael ‘El Mayo’ Zambada García from Mexican territory in 2024 Mexico has launched an investigation into whether the US lied about its involvement in the capture and secretive transfer of a top Sinaloa cartel member in 2024, in what would be a potential violation of the country’s sovereignty. The US has long denied it played any role in the operation to detain the drug lord Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada García, a founder of the Sinaloa cartel, inside Mexico. Recent reporting by the local media outlet Pie de Nota , however, suggested that the FBI was involved in his capture. Continue reading...

events.title: Victorian crossbench MP warns voting system overhaul must be backed by broader reform – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed. Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Victorian Animal Justice party MP Georgie Purcell says abolishing the state’s group voting ticket system without any other changes would see the balance of power handed to One Nation. The Age reported yesterday that the Allan government is planning to introduce legislation, when parliament returns from the winter break, to reform the state’s unusual voting system. Crossbench MPs like myself support electoral reform, and we’ve been asking for it to be implemented in a sensible way for years. We want change too. But we don’t want a model that disproportionately benefits certain minor parties. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Victorian crossbench MP warns voting system overhaul must be backed by broader reform – as it happened

events.title: ‘A lot of red flags’: plans for New Zealand’s first datacentre spark concern as locals demand greater transparency
events.description: Plans to build a NZ$3.5bn datacentre in Makarewa in the country’s south has drawn concern about electricity and water use, and potential noise pollution People living near the site of New Zealand’s first planned AI datacentre are calling for more transparency about the project, especially about how the centre’s huge electricity and water use and potential noise pollution could affect them. Singapore-based company Datagrid has secured approval to build a NZ$3.5bn (US$2bn) AI datacentre on a 49-hectare site in Makarewa, just north of New Zealand’s southern-most city, Invercargill. Construction is due to begin this year, with the centre becoming operational by 2028. Continue reading...

events.description: Trump made the comments on his Truth Social after U.S. officials demanded that Iran make a public statement saying the Strait of Hormuz is open.

events.title: Berlin hostage crisis: Woman freed after hourslong standoff
events.description: A man allegedly took a woman hostage in a supermarket, prompting a major police operation. The suspect reportedly "pulled out" a big knife before the supermarket closed on Friday evening.

events.title: Sudan: ‘El Obeid must not become the next crime scene’ warn UN investigators
events.description: Atrocities committed by the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) militia against Sudanese civilians in El Fasher in October – including mass killings, abductions and gang rapes – bear the markers of genocide, independent UN human rights investigators reported on Wednesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan: ‘El Obeid must not become the next crime scene’ warn UN investigators

events.title: Cause of death revealed for American mother murdered in Ireland: report
events.description: Jamey Carney, 43, reportedly died from suffocation after suffering head injuries at her Killarney home. A person of interest reportedly fled to Istanbul.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Cause of death revealed for American mother murdered in Ireland: report

events.title: Critics say Turkey's verbal attacks on Israel have crossed into antisemitism
events.description: Turkish FM Hakan Fidan reportedly called Israel a burden humanity can no longer bear, drawing accusations of genocidal rhetoric as Turkey hosts a NATO summit in Ankara.

events.title: Meet the crypto billionaires building a world where money buys you a vote
events.description: They think democracy has run its course - so they're setting up their own nations instead.

events.title: How a viral seal called Neil caused mayhem in Australia

events.title: What was the mistaken identity red card shown in Argentina vs Switzerland?

events.title: Argentina beats Switzerland to set up World Cup 2026 semifinal with England
events.description: Argentina need extra time to beat Switzerland 3-1 in the quarterfinals to set up a showdown with England on Wednesday.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Wallabies’ pop-gun revival under Joe Schmidt blown apart as France unload heavy artillery | Daniel Gallan
events.description: Plucky defeats decorated with patches of excellence will not cut it for Australia with a home World Cup now looming large The camera found Joe Schmidt shortly after France had completed a 22-point swing. Australia’s coach had seen a 21-12 half-time lead obliterated in 16 brutal minutes. Schmidt, one of rugby’s sharpest minds, looked short of answers. The trouble was that the questions confronting him had obvious answers but almost impossible solutions. Why had Australia’s discipline deteriorated? Because they were under pressure. Why had their tackle intensity and ruck speed fallen away? Because France had introduced fresh power from the bench. Why had the Wallabies gone from a nine-point half-time lead to a 13-point deficit in barely a quarter of an hour? Because one team had more large, skilful, Test-quality rugby players than the other. Continue reading...

events.title: Spain edges Belgium and will face France in World Cup semifinal
events.description: Spain and France will meet Tuesday in Arlington, Texas, in a matchup anticipated for years. Neither team has lost at this year's World Cup.

events.title: Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei vows revenge for father's killing
events.description: Iran's supreme leader said Iran "demands" retribution for his father's assassination in an airstrike. Meanwhile, Trump has threatened to "completely decimate" Iran should it attempt to assassinate him.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei vows revenge for father's killing

events.title: The week in pictures: Le Pen's comeback, Khamenei's funeral and Mbappé's wondergoal
events.description: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen was cleared to run in next year's presidential election, despite having her embezzlement conviction confirmed, in a week that saw Iran hold days-long funeral ceremonies for slain supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and football stars Kylian Mbappé and Lionel Messi deliver moments of magic to carry their teams into the World Cup semi-finals. FRANCE 24 looks back at some of the week's most striking images.

events.title: US rapper Pitbull sets bald cap world record at London show
events.description: US rapper Pitbull has set the first Guinness World Record for the largest gathering of people wearing bald caps.

events.title: England, Argentina set up World Cup showdown after quarter-final wins
events.description: England and Argentina set up a blockbuster World Cup semi-final showdown on Saturday after battling past Norway and Switzerland to book their passage to the last four. France 24's Selina Sykes tells us more.

events.title: Live: US launches strikes after Iran fires on ship in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The US military launched fresh strikes on Iran after it set a container ship ablaze on Sunday. The defence ministries of the United Arab Emirates and Qatar both said they were intercepting incoming Iranian fire. Read our blog to follow the events as they unfold.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Argentina reach semi-finals after extra-time battle with 10-man Switzerland

events.title: World Cup 2026: Argentina reach semi-finals after extra-time battle with 10-man Switzerland
events.description: Argentina booked their place in the semi-finals with a dramatic 3-1 extra-time victory over Switzerland in the quarter-finals, with the Nati's numerical disadvantage ultimately proving decisive. Despite Embolo's dismissal reducing Switzerland to ten men, the Alpine underdogs fought valiantly before Argentina's greater experience and depth secured passage to the final four.

events.title: Norway eliminated: What do supporters think of their team's overall performance?
events.description: Norway lost to England 2-1 in the additional time, after a tight game in the quarterfinals of the World Cup. We met with Norwegian fans to ask them what their thought of their team's performance during the competition.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Bellingham's brace sends England past Norway to semi-finals
events.description: No description available.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Bellingham's brace sends England past Norway to semi-finals
events.description: England booked their place in the semi-finals with a 2-1 extra-time victory over Norway in the quarter-finals, with Jude Bellingham's brace proving decisive. The Three Lions came from behind to overcome a resilient Norwegian side led by Erling Haaland, progressing after an intense extra-time battle in Miami. They will take on the winner of the Argentina-Switzerland match.

events.title: Norway-England: Three Lions advance to semi-finals after Bellingham's extra-time double
events.description: England advanced to the semi-finals on Saturday with a 2-1 extra-time victory over Norway in the World Cup quarter-finals, with Jude Bellingham's brace proving decisive in Miami. Relive our minute-by-minute commentary to see how things unfolded.

events.title: World must move faster on Ebola response for DR Congo: UN relief chief

events.title: Remembering the Srebrenica genocide: ‘Fear is all that kept me moving’
events.description: The United Nations paused on Thursday to remember the more than 8,000 Bosnian Muslim men and boys killed in the town of Srebrenica in July 1995, and the women and survivors left to rebuild their lives in the aftermath.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color

events.title: UK judges begin hearing appeal over Trinidad and Tobago anti-gay law
events.description: Activist is challenging ruling last year that restored colonial-era homophobic law against same-sex intimacy Some of the UK’s top judges are hearing arguments over whether a Trinidad and Tobago court had the legal right to overturn a 2018 ruling to remove colonial-era homophobic laws that criminalise anal sex between consenting men. The country’s “ buggery law ”, often referred to as its “sodomy” law, was created in 1925 and was written into Trinidad and Tobago’s 1986 Sexual Offences Act . In 2017 a Trinidadian LGBTQ+ rights activist, Jason Jones , challenged the law, and in 2018 a high court ruled that it infringed upon his constitutional right to privacy and equality. Continue reading...

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Germany's Ruhr Valley is on brink of financial collapse

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million

events.title: Germany news: Neuschwanstein killer fights extradition to US
events.description: A man who murdered a woman at Neuschwanstein Castle says he fears execution in the US. Germany has been hit by another heat wave, though no records are likely to be broken this time, forecasters say. DW has more.

events.title: Typhoon Bavi hits China after battering Taiwan, Japan
events.description: Bavi has forced hundreds of thousands to evacuate in China, knocked out power in Japan and Taiwan, and triggered deadly landslides in the Philippines.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Typhoon Bavi hits China after battering Taiwan, Japan

events.title: Germany: Left-wing group says it is behind railway sabotage
events.description: The "Angry Birds Commando" group said they placed incendiary devices on a railroad between the two busy cities of Cologne and Düsseldorf. The group blamed industrial technology for the destruction of the environment.

events.title: India news: US to seek Lawrence Bishnoi's extradition
events.description: US authorities have confirmed that they want to extradite Lawrence Bishnoi, who was charged with the killing of Sikh separatist leader Hardeep ​Singh Nijjar in Canada. Follow DW for more.

events.title: Eiffel Tower to close early as heatwave hits France: operator
events.description: The operator of the Eiffel Tower said the latticed-steel Paris monument would "exceptionally close" early over the weekend at 4 pm (1400 GMT), as France was gripped by a third heatwave since May.

events.title: Lindsey Graham, pivotal US Republican senator, dies suddenly at 71
events.description: US Senator Lindsey Graham, a key Republican who went from a vocal critic of Donald Trump to one of his most loyal allies on Capitol Hill after Trump became president, has died at age 71. Our correspondent Fraser Jackson tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Lindsey Graham, pivotal US Republican senator, dies suddenly at 71

events.title: Argentina-Switzerland: Lautaro Martinez seals Albiceleste's passage to the semi-finals
events.description: Argentina advanced to the semi-finals with a dramatic 3-1 extra-time victory over 10-man Switzerland, with Lautaro Martinez's late goal sealing their passage to face England. Relive our minute-by-minute commentary to see how things unfolded.

events.title: Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies - as Zelenskyy pays tribute
events.description: Lindsey Graham, the US senator and prominent ally of Donald Trump, has died.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies - as Zelenskyy pays tribute

events.title: US congressman 'detained by armed Israeli settlers' in West Bank
events.description: A US politician said he was detained by armed Israeli settlers during a visit to the West Bank.

events.title: What Peter Falconio's killer told police when asked to reveal location of backpacker's body
events.description: New footage has emerged showing the man who murdered British backpacker Peter Falconio repeatedly denying that he knew where his victim's body was.

events.title: Europe's first total solar eclipse in almost 30 years: What you need to know
events.description: Day will briefly turn into night for parts of Europe next month as the continent experiences its first solar eclipse in nearly three decades.

events.title: Japan's space agency conducts first test flight for experimental reusable rocket
events.description: Japan has conducted a first test flight for its reusable rocket

events.title: Focus turns to building stronger institutions in Africa to speed shift to renewable energy
events.description: Africa’s renewable energy transition is entering a new phase as the continent shifts from proving that clean energy works to building the institutions needed to deploy it at scale

events.title: More than 1 million evacuated Typhoon Bavi hits China
events.description: It was the second typhoon in a week to impact China in just over a week.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ More than 1 million evacuated Typhoon Bavi hits China

events.title: Typhoon Bavi brings strong winds and rain to eastern China as more than 2 million people evacuated
events.description: Typhoon Bavi is bringing strong winds and heavy rain to eastern China
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Typhoon Bavi brings strong winds and rain to eastern China as more than 2 million people evacuated

events.title: UK police release man arrested over killing of former politician Ann Widdecombe
events.description: British police have released a 26-year-old man arrested in connection with the killing of former politician Ann Widdecombe

events.title: Mali's military says it's broken a rebel blockade around a strategic northern base
events.description: Mali’s military says it has broken a rebel blockade around a strategic army base in the north as the junta battles a renewed offensive by separatists and al-Qaida-linked militants
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mali's military says it's broken a rebel blockade around a strategic northern base

events.title: Diplomats go virtual to witness Gaza displacement site up close
events.description: Representatives from 12 countries carried out a “virtual diplomatic field visit” to a displacement site in the Gaza Strip and heard from some of the residents about their pressing needs, the United Nations said on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Diplomats go virtual to witness Gaza displacement site up close

events.title: Fox News Campus Radicals Newsletter: Alleged K-12 fraud, one school's battle with Newsom and a win in Florida
events.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News Campus Radicals newsletter.

events.title: NYPD officer charged with rape, child sex crimes previously admitted to misconduct on the job, records show
events.description: NYPD officer Joshua Acosta faces rape and child sex charges after years of documented police misconduct while serving in Brooklyn, documents show.

events.title: Nicole Brown Simpson told former lover she'd 'rather die' than return to O.J. Simpson before her murder
events.description: Joseph Perrulli reveals Nicole Brown Simpson's chilling prediction about O.J. Simpson during their 1992 love affair in "The Forgotten Briefcase."

events.title: Slain New Jersey therapist’s grad school roommate recounts friendship with couple during time in Boston
events.description: A close friend and former roommate of Brooke Hanlon speaks out about the Chester, New Jersey stabbing and what she observed about Conor Hanlon during the couple's relationship.

events.title: Tyler Robinson hearing ends without ruling as evidence dismantles Charlie Kirk conspiracy claims
events.description: Prosecutors presented autopsy results, surveillance footage, and Lance Twiggs' explosive interview as evidence in the Charlie Kirk assassination case.

events.title: New Jersey woman accused of sexually assaulting child, posting video on Snapchat: police
events.description: Victoria Anne Cranmer allegedly recorded a 14-second video of herself sexually assaulting a child under 13 and shared it on Snapchat, police say.

events.title: Yale student charged after alleged sex assault on packed Manhattan subway train, attorney pushes back
events.description: Ari Shtein, 18, faces sexual abuse and forcible touching charges after an alleged assault on a Manhattan subway train, but his attorney calls it false.

events.title: NJ husband begs for CPR help in 911 call after wife found dead with stab wounds: audio
events.description: Conor Hanlon's 911 call captures him frantically requesting CPR instructions after finding wife Brooke Hanlon unconscious and bloodied on their floor.
events.ticker_label: 💥 NJ husband begs for CPR help in 911 call after wife found dead with stab wounds: audio

events.title: Court hears alleged confession from accused Charlie Kirk assassin in texts with lover and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Former British MP and reality TV star Ann Widdecombe found dead; man arrested for murder
events.description: A 26-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of murder after Ann Widdecombe was found dead at her Dartmoor home with serious injuries Thursday.

events.title: US urges donors to abandon UNRWA funding as UN defends agency's mission
events.description: U.S. Ambassador Jeff Bartos accused UNRWA of becoming a "subsidiary of Hamas" and urged donors to fund the Board of Peace instead at the conference.
